---
title: "Soybean Rhizosphere Metagenome Analyses"
author: "Qi Wang (qwang2@mpipz.mpg.de)"
affiliation: "Lab of Paul Schulze-Lefert, Ruben Garrido-Oter, Stephane Hacquard | Department of Plant and microbe Interaction | Max Planck Institute for Plant Breeding Research"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
output: 
  rmdformats::html_clean:
  code_folding: hide
toc: true
thumbnails: false
toc_depth: 3
pkgdown:
  as_is: true
---
  
We have 42 samples that represent the following: 

.WT_0uM_1	0uM	WT	Rep.1
.WT_0uM_2	0uM	WT	Rep.2
.WT_0uM_3	0uM	WT	Rep.3
.WT_100uM_1	100uM	WT	Rep.1
.WT_100uM_2	100uM	WT	Rep.2
.WT_100uM_3	100uM	WT	Rep.3
.WT_10uM_1	10uM	WT	Rep.1
.WT_10uM_2	10uM	WT	Rep.2
.WT_10uM_3	10uM	WT	Rep.3
.WT_200uM_1	200uM	WT	Rep.1
.WT_200uM_2	200uM	WT	Rep.2
.WT_200uM_3	200uM	WT	Rep.3
.WT_20uM_1	20uM	WT	Rep.1
.WT_20uM_2	20uM	WT	Rep.2
.WT_20uM_3	20uM	WT	Rep.3
.WT_50uM_1	50uM	WT	Rep.1
.WT_50uM_2	50uM	WT	Rep.2
.WT_50uM_3	50uM	WT	Rep.3
.WT_Ctl_1	Control	WT	Rep.1
.WT_Ctl_2	Control	WT	Rep.2
.WT_Ctl_3	Control	WT	Rep.3
.phr1_2_0uM_1	0uM	phr1phr2	Rep.1
.phr1_2_0uM_2	0uM	phr1phr2	Rep.2
.phr1_2_0uM_3	0uM	phr1phr2	Rep.3
.phr1_2_100uM_1	100uM	phr1phr2	Rep.1
.phr1_2_100uM_2	100uM	phr1phr2	Rep.2
.phr1_2_100uM_3	100uM	phr1phr2	Rep.3
.phr1_2_10uM_1	10uM	phr1phr2	Rep.1
.phr1_2_10uM_2	10uM	phr1phr2	Rep.2
.phr1_2_10uM_3	10uM	phr1phr2	Rep.3
.phr1_2_200uM_1	200uM	phr1phr2	Rep.1
.phr1_2_200uM_2	200uM	phr1phr2	Rep.2
.phr1_2_200uM_3	200uM	phr1phr2	Rep.3
.phr1_2_20uM_1	20uM	phr1phr2	Rep.1
.phr1_2_20uM_2	20uM	phr1phr2	Rep.2
.phr1_2_20uM_3	20uM	phr1phr2	Rep.3
.phr1_2_50uM_1	50uM	phr1phr2	Rep.1
.phr1_2_50uM_2	50uM	phr1phr2	Rep.2
.phr1_2_50uM_3	50uM	phr1phr2	Rep.3
.phr1_2_Ctl_1	Control	phr1phr2	Rep.1
.phr1_2_Ctl_2	Control	phr1phr2	Rep.2
.phr1_2_Ctl_3	Control	phr1phr2	Rep.3



#Load R packages

```{r}
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)  # includes ggplot2, dplyr, tibble, tidyr, etc.
library(tidyr)
library(scales)     # for percent_format in plots

```



# used tools in bash

```{r}
# Standard shell utilities
mkdir
ls
basename
echo
cat
rm
sleep
sort
tr
sed
awk
find

# Quality control
fastp

# Host removal & read mapping
bowtie2
samtools

# Taxonomic classification & abundance estimation
kraken2
bracken
python
python3  # for Bracken matrix merging

# Coverage normalization
bbnorm.sh

# Assembly & assembly QC
megahit
quast.py

# Sequence retrieval from KEGG
curl

```

# 1 This script performs automated quality control, merging, and host (soybean) read removal on metagenomic sequencing data to generate high-quality, host-free reads for downstream analysis.

  
```{bash, cache=F}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=QC_HostRemoval
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=32
#SBATCH --mem=300G
#SBATCH --time=12:00:00
#SBATCH --output=01_QC_HostRemoval_%j.log

set -euo pipefail

# ===================== CONFIG =====================
THREADS=32
SAMTOOLS_THREADS=24

DATA_DIR="/Data/to/path"
OUT_DIR="/Data/to/path"
NOHOST_DIR="${OUT_DIR}/nohost_reads"

# Bowtie2 index PREFIX (NOT directory)
BT2_DB="/Data/to/path"

mkdir -p "${OUT_DIR}" "${NOHOST_DIR}"

# ===================== MAIN LOOP =====================
for SAMPLE_DIR in "${DATA_DIR}"/*; do
    [[ -d "${SAMPLE_DIR}" ]] || continue

    SAMPLE=$(basename "${SAMPLE_DIR}")
    echo "=============================="
    echo "Processing sample: ${SAMPLE}"

    FILES=("${SAMPLE_DIR}"/*.fq.gz)

    if [[ ${#FILES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "WARNING: No FASTQ files found, skipping ${SAMPLE}"
        continue
    fi

    # ---------- 1. Merge multiple files (single-end) ----------
    MERGED_FASTQ="${OUT_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_merged.fq.gz"
    echo "Merging FASTQ files..."
    cat "${FILES[@]}" > "${MERGED_FASTQ}"

    # ---------- 2. Quality control ----------
    echo "Running fastp..."
    fastp \
        -i "${MERGED_FASTQ}" \
        -o "${OUT_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_qc.fq.gz" \
        --thread ${THREADS} \
        --html "${OUT_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_fastp.html" \
        --json "${OUT_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_fastp.json"

    rm "${MERGED_FASTQ}"

    # ---------- 3. Host removal (soybean) ----------
    echo "Removing host reads..."
    bowtie2 \
        -x "${BT2_DB}" \
        -U "${OUT_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_qc.fq.gz" \
        -p ${THREADS} \
    | samtools view -@ ${SAMTOOLS_THREADS} -b -f 4 - \
    | samtools fastq -@ ${SAMTOOLS_THREADS} - \
    > "${NOHOST_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_nohost.fq.gz"

    rm "${OUT_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_qc.fq.gz"

    echo "Finished sample: ${SAMPLE}"
done

echo "=========================================="
echo "QC + host removal completed for all samples"
```



# 2 This script performs taxonomic classification and abundance estimation of host-free metagenomic reads by running Kraken2 for read classification and Bracken for abundance re-estimation at multiple taxonomic levels, and then merges all results into sample-by-taxon abundance matrices for downstream microbial community analysis.


  
```{bash, cache=F}
#!/bin/bash
#========================================================
# Kraken2 + Bracken pipeline (all taxonomic levels)
# HPC-safe, handles multiple samples in parallel
#========================================================

############################
# Activate Kraken2/Bracken environment
############################
source /opt/share/software/packages/miniconda3-4.12.0/bin/activate \
  /Data/to/path/conda/envs/kraken2

############################
# Define paths
############################
READS_DIR="/Data/to/path"
KRAKEN2_DB="/Data/to/path"
OUTDIR=${READS_DIR}/kraken2_bracken

mkdir -p ${OUTDIR}
cd ${READS_DIR}

############################
# HPC resource settings
############################
THREADS_PER_SAMPLE=20
MAX_PARALLEL_SAMPLES=4   # 4 samples × 20 threads = 80 cores
READ_LEN=150

############################
# Taxonomic levels
############################
TAX_LEVELS=("S" "G" "F" "O" "C" "P")  # Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum

############################
# Run Kraken2 + Bracken for all samples
############################
ls *_nohost.fq.gz | sed 's/_nohost.fq.gz//' | \
xargs -n 1 -P ${MAX_PARALLEL_SAMPLES} -I {} bash -c '
sample="{}"
echo "Processing sample: ${sample}"

# Kraken2
kraken2 \
  --db '"${KRAKEN2_DB}"' \
  --threads '"${THREADS_PER_SAMPLE}"' \
  --confidence 0.1 \
  --report '"${OUTDIR}"'/${sample}.kraken.report \
  --output '"${OUTDIR}"'/${sample}.kraken.out \
  ${sample}_nohost.fq.gz

# Bracken for all taxonomic levels
TAX_LEVELS=("S" "G" "F" "O" "C" "P")
for level in "${TAX_LEVELS[@]}"; do
  bracken \
    -d '"${KRAKEN2_DB}"' \
    -i '"${OUTDIR}"'/${sample}.kraken.report \
    -o '"${OUTDIR}"'/${sample}.bracken_${level}.txt \
    -r '"${READ_LEN}"' \
    -l $level
done
'

############################
# Merge all Bracken outputs into a single matrix per level
############################
COMBINE_SCRIPT=/netscratch/dep_psl/grp_rgo/Qi/Tools/kraken2_db/Bracken/analysis_scripts/combine_bracken_outputs.py

for level in "${TAX_LEVELS[@]}"; do
  echo "Merging Bracken outputs at level: $level"
  python "${COMBINE_SCRIPT}" \
    -i ${OUTDIR}/*bracken_${level}.txt \
    -o ${OUTDIR}/bracken_${level}_matrix.txt
done

echo "All Bracken matrices generated successfully:"
for level in "${TAX_LEVELS[@]}"; do
  echo "${OUTDIR}/bracken_${level}_matrix.txt"
done

```


# 3 This script merges Bracken abundance outputs across all samples into taxon-by-sample matrices at multiple taxonomic levels, using relative abundances (fraction_total_reads) instead of raw read counts, to generate normalized community composition tables for downstream analysis.

```{bash, cache=F, eval=F}
#!/bin/bash
#========================================================
# Merge Bracken outputs using fraction_total_reads
#========================================================

# Folder with Bracken outputs
BRACKEN_DIR="/Data/to/path"

# Output folder (same as Bracken folder)
MERGE_OUT=${BRACKEN_DIR}
mkdir -p ${MERGE_OUT}

# Taxonomic levels
TAX_LEVELS=("S" "G" "F" "O" "C" "P")

for level in "${TAX_LEVELS[@]}"; do
    echo "Merging Bracken outputs at level: ${level}"
    python3 - <<EOF
import pandas as pd
import glob
import os

# Find all Bracken files for this level
files = glob.glob(os.path.join("${BRACKEN_DIR}", f"*bracken_${level}.txt"))
dfs = []

for f in files:
    sample_name = os.path.basename(f).split(".bracken_")[0]
    df = pd.read_csv(f, sep="\t", index_col=0)
    # Use fraction_total_reads instead of new_est_reads
    df = df[["fraction_total_reads"]]
    df.rename(columns={"fraction_total_reads": sample_name}, inplace=True)
    dfs.append(df)

# Merge all samples into a single matrix
if dfs:
    merged = pd.concat(dfs, axis=1)
    merged.fillna(0, inplace=True)
    merged.to_csv(os.path.join("${MERGE_OUT}", f"bracken_${level}_matrix_fraction.txt"), sep="\t")
EOF
done

echo "All merged Bracken matrices (fraction_total_reads) are in ${MERGE_OUT}"

```



#4 PCoA_plot_filtered

```{r}

############################
# 1. Load libraries
############################
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(vegan)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(readr)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tibble)
  library(readxl)
})

############################
# 2. Input files
############################
bracken_file  <- "/Data/to/path"
metadata_file <- "/Data/to/path"

############################
# 3. Load data
############################
abund <- read_tsv(bracken_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
meta  <- read_excel(metadata_file, sheet = "Sheet1") %>%
  select(SampleID, Genotype, Treatment)

############################
# 4. Prepare abundance matrix
# Rows = taxa, Columns = samples
############################
abund_mat <- abund %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = colnames(abund)[1]) %>%
  as.matrix()
abund_mat <- t(abund_mat)  # samples × taxa

############################
# 5. Filter low-abundance taxa
# Keep taxa with mean relative abundance > 0.01%
############################
abund_mat <- abund_mat[, colMeans(abund_mat) > 0.0001]

############################
# 6. Hellinger transformation
############################
abund_hel <- decostand(abund_mat, method = "hellinger")

############################
# 7. Match metadata to abundance matrix
############################
meta <- meta %>%
  filter(SampleID %in% rownames(abund_hel)) %>%
  arrange(match(SampleID, rownames(abund_hel)))

if (!all(meta$SampleID == rownames(abund_hel))) {
  stop("Sample order mismatch between metadata and abundance matrix.")
}

############################
# 8. Bray–Curtis distance
############################
bray_dist <- vegdist(abund_hel, method = "bray")

############################
# 9. PCoA
############################
pcoa_res <- cmdscale(bray_dist, eig = TRUE, k = 2)
var_exp <- round(100 * pcoa_res$eig / sum(pcoa_res$eig), 1)

pcoa_df <- data.frame(
  SampleID = rownames(abund_hel),
  PC1 = pcoa_res$points[, 1],
  PC2 = pcoa_res$points[, 2]
) %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "SampleID")

write.table(
  pcoa_df,
  file = "PCoA_coordinates_filtered_hellinger.tsv",
  sep = "\t",
  quote = FALSE,
  row.names = FALSE
)

############################
# 10. Colors and shapes
############################
color_values <- c("#BE4BDB", "#228BE6")      # Genotype colors
shape_values <- c(16, 17, 15, 3, 7, 8, 0)   # Treatment shapes

# Ensure Genotype has exactly 2 levels
pcoa_df$Genotype <- factor(pcoa_df$Genotype)

# Custom Treatment order for legend
treatment_order <- c(
  "Ctl",
  "Brady_0uM",
  "Brady_10uM",
  "Brady_20uM",
  "Brady_50uM",
  "Brady_100uM",
  "Brady_200uM"
)
pcoa_df$Treatment <- factor(pcoa_df$Treatment, levels = treatment_order)

############################
# 11. PCoA plot
############################
p <- ggplot(
  pcoa_df,
  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Genotype, shape = Treatment)
) +
  geom_point(size = 4, stroke = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = color_values) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = shape_values) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PCoA1 (", var_exp[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PCoA2 (", var_exp[2], "%)"),
    color = "Genotype",
    shape = "Treatment"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(size = 0.5),
    text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm")
  )

print(p)

ggsave(
  "PCoA_plot_filtered_hellinger_custom_legend.pdf",
  plot = p,
  width = 7,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

############################
# 12. PERMANOVA
############################
cat("\n=== PERMANOVA: Genotype ===\n")
perm_genotype <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ Genotype, data = meta, permutations = 999)
print(perm_genotype)

cat("\n=== PERMANOVA: Treatment ===\n")
perm_treatment <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ Treatment, data = meta, permutations = 999)
print(perm_treatment)

cat("\n=== PERMANOVA: Genotype × Treatment ===\n")
perm_interaction <- adonis2(bray_dist ~ Genotype * Treatment, data = meta, permutations = 999)
print(perm_interaction)

############################
# 13. Dispersion test (PERMDISP)
############################
cat("\n=== Homogeneity of dispersion (Genotype) ===\n")
disp <- betadisper(bray_dist, meta$Genotype)
print(anova(disp))

############################
# 14. Save PERMANOVA results
############################
sink("PERMANOVA_results_filtered_hellinger_custom_legend.txt")
cat("PERMANOVA: Genotype\n")
print(perm_genotype)
cat("\nPERMANOVA: Treatment\n")
print(perm_treatment)
cat("\nPERMANOVA: Genotype × Treatment\n")
print(perm_interaction)
cat("\nDispersion test (Genotype)\n")
print(anova(disp))
sink()

cat("\nAnalysis completed successfully.\n")




```


## 5 This R script produces a family-level relative abundance stacked bar plot from Bracken output.


```{r}


############################
# Load required libraries
############################
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(tibble)
})

############################
# Input file
############################
bracken_file <- "/Data/to/path"

############################
# Read Bracken data
# Rows = Family, Columns = Samples
# Remove WT_10uM_2
############################
abund <- read_tsv(
  bracken_file,
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  select(-WT_10uM_2)

############################
# Set Family names as rownames
############################
abund_mat <- abund %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = colnames(abund)[1]) %>%
  as.matrix()

############################
# Convert to relative abundance
############################
abund_rel <- abund_mat / colSums(abund_mat)

############################
# Long format
############################
df_long <- as.data.frame(abund_rel) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Family") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Family,
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "Abundance"
  )

############################
# Collapse replicates by mean
############################
df_long <- df_long %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_group = str_remove(Sample, "_[1-3]$")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Sample_group, Family) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = mean(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

############################
# Identify top 20 families (global mean)
############################
top20_families <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Family) %>%
  summarise(mean_abund = mean(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_abund)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(Family)

############################
# Collapse rare families to "Others"
############################
df_long <- df_long %>%
  mutate(
    Family = ifelse(Family %in% top20_families, Family, "Others")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Sample_group, Family) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

############################
# Set factor levels (critical for colors)
############################
df_long$Family <- factor(
  df_long$Family,
  levels = c(top20_families, "Others")
)

############################
# Define Sample order
############################
sample_order <- c(
  "WT_Ctl", "WT_0uM", "WT_10uM", "WT_20uM", "WT_50uM",
  "WT_100uM", "WT_200uM",
  "phr1_2_Ctl", "phr1_2_0uM", "phr1_2_10uM", "phr1_2_20uM",
  "phr1_2_50uM", "phr1_2_100uM", "phr1_2_200uM"
)

df_long$Sample_group <- factor(
  df_long$Sample_group,
  levels = sample_order
)

############################
# QIIME2-style color palette (20 families)
############################
qiime2_colors <- c(
  "#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd",
  "#8c564b", "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f", "#bcbd22", "#17becf",
  "#aec7e8", "#ffbb78", "#98df8a", "#ff9896", "#c5b0d5",
  "#c49c94", "#f7b6d2", "#c7c7c7", "#dbdb8d", "#9edae5"
)

family_colors <- setNames(qiime2_colors, top20_families)
family_colors <- c(family_colors, Others = "gray50")

############################
# Plot stacked bar chart
############################
p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Sample_group, y = Abundance, fill = Family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = family_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Relative abundance (%)",
    fill = "Family"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.line    = element_line(size = 0.5),
    text         = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.3, "cm")
  )



print(p)

############################################################
# End of script
############################################################


```


## 6 This R script produces a genus-level relative abundance stacked bar plot from Bracken output, with several careful data preprocessing steps.



```{r}
############################
# Load required libraries
############################
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(tibble)
})

############################
# Input file
############################
bracken_file <- "/Data/to/path"

############################
# Read Bracken data
# Rows = Genus, Columns = Samples
# Remove WT_10uM_2
############################
abund <- read_tsv(
  bracken_file,
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  select(-WT_10uM_2)

############################
# Set Genus names as rownames
############################
abund_mat <- abund %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = colnames(abund)[1]) %>%
  as.matrix()

############################
# Convert to relative abundance
############################
abund_rel <- abund_mat / colSums(abund_mat)

############################
# Long format
############################
df_long <- as.data.frame(abund_rel) %>%
  rownames_to_column("Genus") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Genus,
    names_to = "Sample",
    values_to = "Abundance"
  )

############################
# Collapse replicates by mean
############################
df_long <- df_long %>%
  mutate(
    Sample_group = str_remove(Sample, "_[1-3]$")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Sample_group, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = mean(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

############################
# Identify top 20 genera (global mean)
############################
top20_genera <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(mean_abund = mean(Abundance), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean_abund)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(Genus)

############################
# Collapse rare genera to "Others"
############################
df_long <- df_long %>%
  mutate(
    Genus = ifelse(Genus %in% top20_genera, Genus, "Others")
  ) %>%
  group_by(Sample_group, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

############################
# Set factor levels (critical for colors)
############################
df_long$Genus <- factor(
  df_long$Genus,
  levels = c(top20_genera, "Others")
)

############################
# Define Sample order
############################
sample_order <- c(
  "WT_Ctl", "WT_0uM", "WT_10uM", "WT_20uM", "WT_50uM",
  "WT_100uM", "WT_200uM",
  "phr1_2_Ctl", "phr1_2_0uM", "phr1_2_10uM", "phr1_2_20uM",
  "phr1_2_50uM", "phr1_2_100uM", "phr1_2_200uM"
)

df_long$Sample_group <- factor(
  df_long$Sample_group,
  levels = sample_order
)

############################
# QIIME2-style color palette (20 genera)
############################
qiime2_colors <- c(
  "#1f77b4", "#ff7f0e", "#2ca02c", "#d62728", "#9467bd",
  "#8c564b", "#e377c2", "#7f7f7f", "#bcbd22", "#17becf",
  "#aec7e8", "#ffbb78", "#98df8a", "#ff9896", "#c5b0d5",
  "#c49c94", "#f7b6d2", "#c7c7c7", "#dbdb8d", "#9edae5"
)

genus_colors <- setNames(qiime2_colors, top20_genera)
genus_colors <- c(genus_colors, Others = "gray50")

############################
# Plot stacked bar chart
############################
p <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Sample_group, y = Abundance, fill = Genus)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.9, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genus_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Relative abundance (%)",
    fill = "Genus"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
    axis.line    = element_line(size = 0.5),
    text         = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.key.size = unit(0.3, "cm")
  )




print(p)

############################################################
# End of script
############################################################





```



#7. Shannon diversity
```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
############################################################
# Genus-level Shannon diversity analysis
# Mean ± SE barplot + replicate jitter + WT vs phr1_2 stars
# Metadata: Brady_* treatments, phr1phr2 genotype
# Author: Qi
############################################################

############################
# Load libraries
############################
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(tibble)
  library(readxl)
})

############################
# Input files
############################
bracken_file <- "/Data/to/path"
metadata_file <- "/Data/to/path"

############################
# Read Bracken genus table
############################
abund <- read_tsv(bracken_file, show_col_types = FALSE)

############################
# Read metadata
############################
meta_raw <- read_excel(metadata_file, sheet = "Sheet1") %>%
  select(SampleID, Treatment, Genotype)

############################
# Recode metadata
############################
meta <- meta_raw %>%
  mutate(
    Treatment = case_when(
      Treatment == "Ctl" ~ "Ctl",
      Treatment == "Brady_0uM"   ~ "0uM",
      Treatment == "Brady_10uM"  ~ "10uM",
      Treatment == "Brady_20uM"  ~ "20uM",
      Treatment == "Brady_50uM"  ~ "50uM",
      Treatment == "Brady_100uM" ~ "100uM",
      Treatment == "Brady_200uM" ~ "200uM"
    ),
    Genotype = ifelse(Genotype == "phr1phr2", "phr1_2", Genotype)
  )

############################
# Set genus names as rownames
############################
abund_mat <- abund %>%
  column_to_rownames(var = colnames(abund)[1]) %>%
  as.matrix()

############################
# Convert to relative abundance
############################
abund_rel <- abund_mat / colSums(abund_mat)

############################
# Shannon diversity function
############################
shannon <- function(p) {
  p <- p[p > 0]
  -sum(p * log(p))
}

############################
# Calculate Shannon per sample
############################
shannon_df <- data.frame(
  Sample  = colnames(abund_rel),
  Shannon = apply(abund_rel, 2, shannon)
)

############################
# Merge metadata
############################
shannon_df <- shannon_df %>%
  left_join(meta, by = c("Sample" = "SampleID"))

############################
# Set factor order
############################
shannon_df <- shannon_df %>%
  mutate(
    Treatment = factor(Treatment,
                       levels = c("Ctl","0uM","10uM","20uM","50uM","100uM","200uM")),
    Genotype = factor(Genotype,
                      levels = c("WT","phr1_2"))
  )

############################
# Summarize replicates
############################
shannon_summary <- shannon_df %>%
  group_by(Genotype, Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(Shannon),
    SD   = sd(Shannon),
    SE   = SD / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

############################
# WT vs phr1_2 statistics per treatment
############################
stat_df <- shannon_df %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    p.value = tryCatch(t.test(Shannon ~ Genotype)$p.value,
                       error = function(e) NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    signif = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE           ~ "ns"
    )
  )

############################
# Y positions for stars
############################
y_pos <- shannon_summary %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(y = max(Mean + SE) * 1.10, .groups = "drop")

stat_df <- stat_df %>%
  left_join(y_pos, by = "Treatment")

############################
# Genotype color palette
############################
genotype_cols <- c(
  "WT"      = "#228BE6",   # blue
  "phr1_2"  = "#BE4BDB"    # red
)


p_bar <- ggplot() +

  # Bars (mean ± SE, no fill)
  geom_col(
    data = shannon_summary,
    aes(x = Treatment, y = Mean, group = Genotype),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width = 0.6,
    fill = "white",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +

  # Error bars
  geom_errorbar(
    data = shannon_summary,
    aes(x = Treatment, ymin = Mean - SE, ymax = Mean + SE, group = Genotype),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +

  # Jittered replicates (colored by genotype)
  geom_jitter(
    data = shannon_df,
    aes(x = Treatment, y = Shannon, color = Genotype, shape = Genotype),
    position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.15, dodge.width = 0.7),
    size = 2,
    alpha = 0.8
  ) +

  # Significance stars
  geom_text(
    data = stat_df,
    aes(x = Treatment, y = y, label = signif),
    size = 4,
    vjust = 0
  ) +

  # Custom genotype colors (jitter + legend)
  scale_color_manual(values = genotype_cols) +

  labs(
    x = "Pi treatment",
    y = "Shannon diversity (Genus level)",
    color = "Genotype",
    shape = "Genotype"
  ) +

  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.line = element_line(size = 0.5),
    text = element_text(size = 10)
  )

print(p_bar)




############################################################
# End of script
############################################################







```
  
# 8 This script performs coverage normalization of host-free metagenomic reads using BBnorm in parallel on an HPC cluster, generating normalized read files for downstream metagenome assembly (MAG reconstruction).
  
  
```{bash, cache=F, eval=F}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -J normalize_samples
#SBATCH --array=1-42%14
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=60
#SBATCH --mem=500G
#SBATCH -t 48:00:00
#SBATCH -p normal
#SBATCH -o /Data/to/path/logs/norm_%A_%a.out
#SBATCH -e /Data/to/path/logs/norm_%A_%a.err
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --mail-user=user@email.com

set -euo pipefail
trap 'echo "[ERROR] Task ${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID} failed on $(hostname) at $(date)" >&2' ERR

THREADS=${SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK}
BBNORM="/opt/share/software/bin/bbnorm.sh"
INPUT_DIR="/Data/to/path"
OUTPUT_BASE="/Data/to/path"
mkdir -p "${OUTPUT_BASE}"

# Generate sample list once
SAMPLE_LIST="${OUTPUT_BASE}/sample_list.txt"
if [ "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}" -eq 1 ]; then
    ls "${INPUT_DIR}"/*_nohost.fq | sort > "${SAMPLE_LIST}"
fi
while [ ! -f "${SAMPLE_LIST}" ]; do sleep 5; done

# Select sample
INPUT_FILE=$(sed -n "${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p" "${SAMPLE_LIST}")
SAMPLE_NAME=$(basename "${INPUT_FILE}" .fq)
SAMPLE_OUT="${OUTPUT_BASE}/${SAMPLE_NAME}"
mkdir -p "${SAMPLE_OUT}"

echo "[INFO] Normalizing ${SAMPLE_NAME}..."
bbnorm.sh in="${INPUT_FILE}" out="${SAMPLE_OUT}/${SAMPLE_NAME}_norm.fq" target=100 min=5 threads="${THREADS}"
echo "[INFO] Done: ${SAMPLE_NAME}"

```



## 9 This script performs co-assembly of all normalized metagenomic reads using MEGAHIT, followed by assembly quality assessment with QUAST, to generate and evaluate contigs for downstream metagenome-assembled genome (MAG) reconstruction.

```{bash, cache=F, eval=F}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -J MEGAHIT_bbnorm
#SBATCH -p normal
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=100
#SBATCH --mem=800G
#SBATCH -o /Data/to/path/logs/megahit_bbnorm_%j.out
#SBATCH -e /Data/to/path/logs/megahit_bbnorm_%j.err
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --mail-user=user@email.com

set -euo pipefail
trap 'echo "[ERROR] MEGAHIT failed at $(date)" >&2' ERR

# =============================
# Paths
# =============================
NORM_READS="/Data/to/path"
OUT_BASE="/Data/to/path"
ASSEMBLY_DIR="${OUT_BASE}/assembly"
QUAST_DIR="${OUT_BASE}/quast"

mkdir -p "${ASSEMBLY_DIR}" "${QUAST_DIR}"

# =============================
# Collect normalized reads (recursive)
# =============================
echo "[INFO] Collecting normalized FASTQ files"
READS=$(find "${NORM_READS}" -type f -name "*.fq" | sort)

if [[ -z "${READS}" ]]; then
    echo "[ERROR] No normalized FASTQ files found" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "[INFO] Normalized FASTQ files found: $(echo "${READS}" | wc -l)"

# =============================
# MEGAHIT co-assembly
# =============================
echo "[INFO] Starting MEGAHIT with BBNorm reads"

megahit \
    -r $(echo "${READS}" | tr '\n' ',') \
    -o "${ASSEMBLY_DIR}" \
    --force \
    -t ${SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK} \
    --min-contig-len 1000

echo "[INFO] MEGAHIT finished"

# =============================
# QUAST QC
# =============================
echo "[INFO] Running QUAST"

quast.py \
    "${ASSEMBLY_DIR}/final.contigs.fa" \
    -o "${QUAST_DIR}" \
    -t ${SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK}

echo "[INFO] Assembly + QC completed at $(date)"

```

#10 This script downloads amino acid sequences for KEGG Orthology (KO) identifiers listed in a file, fetching up to 200 genes per KO in batches via the KEGG REST API, and saves each KO’s sequences as a separate FASTA file for downstream analyses.

```{bash, cache=F, eval=F}
#!/bin/bash

KO_FILE="/Data/to/path"
OUTDIR="/Data/to/path"
mkdir -p "$OUTDIR"

KEGG="https://rest.kegg.jp"
MAX_GENES_PER_KO=200
BATCH_SIZE=10
DELAY=3

while IFS= read -r line; do
    # --- CLEAN THE LINE ---
    # Remove carriage returns (\r), leading/trailing whitespace, and comments
    KO=$(echo "$line" | tr -d '\r' | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//; s/[[:space:]]*#.*$//; s/[[:space:]]*$//')
    [[ -z "$KO" ]] && continue

    # Validate format: must be K followed by exactly 5 digits
    if ! [[ "$KO" =~ ^K[0-9]{5}$ ]]; then
        echo " ⚠ Skipping invalid KO: '$KO' (expected format: K00001)"
        continue
    fi

    echo "Processing $KO ..."
    OUTFILE="${OUTDIR}/${KO}.faa"

    if [[ -s "$OUTFILE" ]]; then
        echo "   ✓ Already downloaded, skipping"
        continue
    fi

    # Fetch gene list
    GENE_RESPONSE=$(curl -s --user-agent "KEGG-Download/1.0 (qi@mpipz.mpg.de)" "${KEGG}/link/genes/${KO}")
    if [[ -z "$GENE_RESPONSE" ]] || [[ "$GENE_RESPONSE" == *"404 Not Found"* ]]; then
        echo "   ✗ No genes found for $KO"
        continue
    fi

    mapfile -t GENE_ARRAY < <(echo "$GENE_RESPONSE" | cut -f2 | head -n "$MAX_GENES_PER_KO")
    TOTAL_GENES="${#GENE_ARRAY[@]}"

    if [[ $TOTAL_GENES -eq 0 ]]; then
        echo "   ✗ No valid genes for $KO"
        continue
    fi

    echo "   Found $TOTAL_GENES genes"

    > "$OUTFILE"
    BATCH=()
    for GENE in "${GENE_ARRAY[@]}"; do
        BATCH+=("$GENE")
        if [[ ${#BATCH[@]} -eq $BATCH_SIZE ]]; then
            BATCH_STR=$(IFS='+'; echo "${BATCH[*]}")
            curl -s --user-agent "KEGG-Download/1.0 (qi@mpipz.mpg.de)" \
                 "${KEGG}/get/${BATCH_STR}/aaseq" >> "$OUTFILE"
            sleep "$DELAY"
            BATCH=()
        fi
    done

    if [[ ${#BATCH[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
        BATCH_STR=$(IFS='+'; echo "${BATCH[*]}")
        curl -s --user-agent "KEGG-Download/1.0 (qi@mpipz.mpg.de)" \
             "${KEGG}/get/${BATCH_STR}/aaseq" >> "$OUTFILE"
        sleep "$DELAY"
    fi

    if [[ -s "$OUTFILE" ]]; then
        echo " ✓ Success: $KO"
    else
        echo " ✗ Failed: $KO (empty file)"
        rm -f "$OUTFILE"
    fi

done < "$KO_FILE"

echo "All done."

```

## 11 This script reduces redundancy in KO amino acid FASTA files by clustering sequences at 99% identity using CD-HIT, producing non-redundant FASTA files and logging the clustering results.

```{bash, cache=F, eval=F}
#!/bin/bash

IN_DIR="KO_OA_FASTA"
OUT_DIR="fasta_nr"
LOG_DIR="logs"

mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR" "$LOG_DIR"

for FAA in ${IN_DIR}/K*.faa; do

  BASENAME=$(basename "$FAA" .faa)
  OUTFAA="${OUT_DIR}/${BASENAME}.nr.faa"
  LOG="${LOG_DIR}/${BASENAME}.cdhit.log"

  echo "Running CD-HIT on ${BASENAME}"

  cd-hit \
    -i "$FAA" \
    -o "$OUTFAA" \
    -c 0.99 \
    -n 5 \
    -M 16000 \
    -T 8 \
    > "$LOG"

done

echo "CD-HIT finished for all KOs."


```



## 12 This script creates non-redundant KO FASTA files by simply copying each original KO FASTA to a new folder, while reporting the number of sequences in each file, without performing any deduplication.

```{bash, cache=F, eval=F}
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "[INFO] Job started at $(date)"
echo "[INFO] Running on host: $(hostname)"

# ============================================================
# Paths
# ============================================================
SRC_DIR="/Data/to/path"
OUT_DIR="/Data/to/path"

mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR" logs

# ============================================================
# Loop over each KO FASTA
# ============================================================
for FAA in "$SRC_DIR"/K*.faa; do
    KO=$(basename "$FAA" .faa)
    NR_FASTA="${OUT_DIR}/${KO}.nr.faa"

    echo "============================================"
    echo "[INFO] Processing $KO"
    echo "============================================"

    # Copy the original sequences to .nr.faa
    # (keep all sequences, no deduplication)
    cp "$FAA" "$NR_FASTA"

    # Count sequences
    NSEQ=$(grep -c "^>" "$NR_FASTA")
    echo "[INFO] $KO contains $NSEQ sequences"

done

echo "[INFO] All KO .nr.faa files created in $OUT_DIR"
echo "[INFO] Job finished at $(date)"



```



# 13 This script builds HMM profiles for each KO by: aligning non-redundant sequences with MAFFT, trimming the alignment with trimAl, constructing the HMM using hmmbuild, and adding a KO-specific name to the HMM, while skipping KOs with too few sequences.

  
```{bash, eval=F, cache=F}
≈#!/bin/bash

# ============================================================
# Configuration
# ============================================================
NR_DIR="fasta_nr"
ALN_DIR="alignments"
TRIM_DIR="trimmed"
HMM_DIR="hmm"
LOG_DIR="logs"

CPU=8

mkdir -p "$ALN_DIR" "$TRIM_DIR" "$HMM_DIR" "$LOG_DIR"

# ============================================================
# Loop over each KO
# ============================================================
for FAA in ${NR_DIR}/K*.nr.faa; do

  KO=$(basename "$FAA" .nr.faa)

  echo "============================================"
  echo "Processing $KO"
  echo "============================================"

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # Skip if too few sequences
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  NSEQ=$(grep -c "^>" "$FAA")

  if [[ $NSEQ -lt 5 ]]; then
    echo "Skipping $KO (only $NSEQ sequences)" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/skipped.log"
    continue
  fi

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # 1. MAFFT alignment
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  ALN="${ALN_DIR}/${KO}.aln.faa"

  mafft \
    --maxiterate 1000 \
    --localpair \
    --thread $CPU \
    "$FAA" > "$ALN" \
    2> "$LOG_DIR/${KO}.mafft.log"

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # 2. trimAl
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  TRIM="${TRIM_DIR}/${KO}.trim.faa"

  trimal \
    -in "$ALN" \
    -out "$TRIM" \
    -automated1 \
    2> "$LOG_DIR/${KO}.trimal.log"

  # Check trimmed alignment size
  NTRIM=$(grep -c "^>" "$TRIM")

  if [[ $NTRIM -lt 5 ]]; then
    echo "Skipping $KO after trimming (only $NTRIM seqs)" | tee -a "$LOG_DIR/skipped.log"
    continue
  fi

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # 3. Build HMM
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  HMM="${HMM_DIR}/${KO}.hmm"

  hmmbuild \
    --cpu $CPU \
    "$HMM" \
    "$TRIM" \
    > "$LOG_DIR/${KO}.hmmbuild.log"

  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  # 4. Add NAME field explicitly (important)
  # ----------------------------------------------------------
  sed -i "s/^NAME.*/NAME  ${KO}/" "$HMM"

done

echo "All individual HMMs built."

```

# 14 This script merges individual KO HMM files into a single HMM database, checks for duplicate NAME fields, and prepares the database for HMMER searches using hmmpress.

  
```{bash, eval=F, cache=F}
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

# =========================
# Fixed paths (user-specified)
# =========================
HMM_DIR="/Data/to/path"
OUT_DB="/Data/to/path"

# =========================
# Checks
# =========================
if [[ ! -d "$HMM_DIR" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: Directory not found: $HMM_DIR" >&2
    exit 1
fi

command -v hmmpress >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
    echo "ERROR: hmmpress not found in PATH" >&2
    exit 1
}

shopt -s nullglob
HMM_FILES=("$HMM_DIR"/*.hmm)
shopt -u nullglob

if [[ ${#HMM_FILES[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: No .hmm files found in $HMM_DIR" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "Found ${#HMM_FILES[@]} HMM files."

# =========================
# Merge HMM files
# =========================
echo "Merging HMM files into:"
echo "  $OUT_DB"
cat "${HMM_FILES[@]}" > "$OUT_DB"

# =========================
# Validate HMM NAME fields
# =========================
echo "Checking for duplicate HMM NAME entries..."
dup_names=$(grep "^NAME" "$OUT_DB" | awk '{print $2}' | sort | uniq -d)

if [[ -n "$dup_names" ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: Duplicate HMM NAME fields detected:"
    echo "$dup_names"
else
    echo "No duplicate HMM NAME fields found."
fi

# =========================
# Press the HMMER database
# =========================
echo "Running hmmpress..."
hmmpress "$OUT_DB"

echo "Done. HMMER database files created:"
ls -lh "$OUT_DB" "$OUT_DB".h3*



```


# 15 This script computes TPM (Transcripts Per Million) abundances for HMM-identified genes in metagenomic samples by: predicting genes with Prodigal, searching proteins against a merged HMM database with HMMER, extracting hit sequences, mapping raw reads to hit genes using Bowtie2, counting mapped reads, calculating gene lengths, and generating a gene-by-sample TPM abundance matrix.


  
```{bash, eval=F, cache=F}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -J HMMER_gene_TPM
#SBATCH -p normal
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=64
#SBATCH --mem=500G
#SBATCH -t 72:00:00
#SBATCH -o /Data/to/path/logs/hmmer_tpm_%j.out
#SBATCH -e /Data/to/path/logs/hmmer_tpm_%j.err
#SBATCH --mail-type=END,FAIL
#SBATCH --mail-user=user@email.com

set -euo pipefail
shopt -s nullglob

echo "[INFO] Job started at $(date)"
echo "[INFO] Host: $(hostname)"

# =============================
# Paths
# =============================
OUT_BASE="/Data/to/path"
ASSEMBLY="/Data/to/path"
RAW_READS="/Data/to/path"
HMM_FILE="/Data/to/path"

PRED_DIR="${OUT_BASE}/predicted"
SEARCH_DIR="${OUT_BASE}/search"
MAP_DIR="${OUT_BASE}/mapping"
COUNTS_DIR="${OUT_BASE}/counts"
ABUND_DIR="${OUT_BASE}/abundance"

mkdir -p "${PRED_DIR}" "${SEARCH_DIR}" "${MAP_DIR}" \
         "${COUNTS_DIR}" "${ABUND_DIR}"

# =============================
# Validate FASTQ files
# =============================
FASTQS=("${RAW_READS}"/*.fq.gz)
if [[ ${#FASTQS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "[ERROR] No FASTQ files found"
    exit 1
fi

# =============================
# 1. Prodigal gene prediction
# =============================
PROTEINS="${PRED_DIR}/proteins.faa"
GENES="${PRED_DIR}/genes.fna"

if [[ ! -f "${PROTEINS}" ]]; then
    echo "[INFO] Running Prodigal..."
    prodigal -i "${ASSEMBLY}" \
             -a "${PROTEINS}" \
             -d "${GENES}" \
             -p meta -q
fi

# =============================
# 2. HMMER search
# =============================
HITS_TBL="${SEARCH_DIR}/gene_hits.tbl"
DOM_TBL="${SEARCH_DIR}/gene_dom.tbl"

if [[ ! -f "${HITS_TBL}" ]]; then
    echo "[INFO] Running hmmsearch..."
    hmmsearch --cpu "${SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK}" \
              --tblout "${HITS_TBL}" \
              --domtblout "${DOM_TBL}" \
              -E 1e-10 \
              "${HMM_FILE}" "${PROTEINS}" \
              > "${SEARCH_DIR}/search.out"
fi

# =============================
# 3. Extract HMM-hit genes (FIXED)
# =============================
HIT_IDS="${SEARCH_DIR}/hit_ids.txt"
HIT_GENES="${SEARCH_DIR}/hit_genes.fna"

if [[ ! -f "${HIT_GENES}" ]]; then
    echo "[INFO] Extracting HMM hits..."

    # Correct full-sequence E-value column (column 7)
    awk 'NR>3 && $7 < 1e-10 {print $1}' "${HITS_TBL}" | sort -u > "${HIT_IDS}"

    if [[ ! -s "${HIT_IDS}" ]]; then
        echo "[ERROR] No HMM hits found"
        exit 1
    fi

    seqkit grep -f "${HIT_IDS}" "${GENES}" > "${HIT_GENES}"
fi

# =============================
# 4. Bowtie2 index
# =============================
HIT_INDEX="${MAP_DIR}/hit_genes"

if [[ ! -f "${HIT_INDEX}.1.bt2" ]]; then
    echo "[INFO] Building Bowtie2 index..."
    bowtie2-build "${HIT_GENES}" "${HIT_INDEX}"
fi

# =============================
# 5. Mapping and counting (FIXED)
# =============================
for FQ in "${FASTQS[@]}"; do
    SAMPLE=$(basename "${FQ}" .fq.gz)
    BAM="${MAP_DIR}/${SAMPLE}.bam"
    COUNTS="${COUNTS_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_counts.tsv"

    if [[ ! -f "${BAM}" ]]; then
        echo "[INFO] Mapping ${SAMPLE}..."
        bowtie2 -p "${SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK}" \
                -x "${HIT_INDEX}" \
                -U "${FQ}" |
            samtools sort -@ "${SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK}" -o "${BAM}" -
        samtools index "${BAM}"
    fi

    if [[ ! -f "${COUNTS}" ]]; then
        echo "[INFO] Counting reads for ${SAMPLE}..."
        samtools idxstats "${BAM}" |
            awk '$1!="*" {print $1 "\t" $3}' > "${COUNTS}"
    fi
done

# =============================
# 6. Gene length calculation (FIXED)
# =============================
GENE_LEN_FILE="${COUNTS_DIR}/hit_gene_lengths.tsv"

awk '
    /^>/{
        if(id){
            print id, length(seq)
        }
        split($0,a," ")
        id=substr(a[1],2)
        seq=""
        next
    }
    {seq=seq $0}
    END{
        if(id){
            print id, length(seq)
        }
    }
' "${HIT_GENES}" > "${GENE_LEN_FILE}"

if [[ ! -s "${GENE_LEN_FILE}" ]]; then
    echo "[ERROR] Gene length file empty"
    exit 1
fi

# =============================
# 7. TPM calculation (FINAL)
# =============================
TPM_MATRIX="${ABUND_DIR}/gene_abundance_tpm.tsv"

echo "[INFO] Calculating TPM..."

{
    printf "Gene_ID"
    for FQ in "${FASTQS[@]}"; do
        printf "\t%s" "$(basename "${FQ}" .fq.gz)"
    done
    printf "\n"

    while read -r GENE LEN; do
        printf "%s" "${GENE}"

        for FQ in "${FASTQS[@]}"; do
            SAMPLE=$(basename "${FQ}" .fq.gz)
            COUNTS_FILE="${COUNTS_DIR}/${SAMPLE}_counts.tsv"

            COUNT=$(awk -v g="${GENE}" '$1==g {print $2; found=1} END{if(!found) print 0}' "${COUNTS_FILE}")
            RPK=$(awk -v c="${COUNT}" -v l="${LEN}" 'BEGIN{print (l>0)?c/(l/1000):0}')
            printf "\t%f" "${RPK}"
        done
        printf "\n"
    done < "${GENE_LEN_FILE}" |
    awk '
        BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t"}
        NR==1{header=$0; next}
        {
            genes[NR]=$1
            for(i=2;i<=NF;i++){
                rpk[NR,i]=$i
                sum[i]+=$i
            }
        }
        END{
            print header
            for(r=2;r<=NR;r++){
                printf "%s", genes[r]
                for(i=2;i<=NF;i++){
                    tpm=(sum[i]>0)?rpk[r,i]/sum[i]*1e6:0
                    printf OFS "%.6f", tpm
                }
                print ""
            }
        }
    '
} > "${TPM_MATRIX}"

echo "[INFO] TPM matrix written to ${TPM_MATRIX}"
echo "[INFO] Job completed at $(date)"



```



# 16 This script annotates a gene-level TPM matrix with KEGG Orthology (KO) identifiers by mapping each gene to its KO based on HMMER hit results and producing a KO-tagged TPM matrix for downstream functional analysis.


  
```{bash, eval=F, cache=F}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
export LC_ALL=C

TPM_MATRIX="/Data/to/path"
GENE_HITS="/Data/to/path"
OUT_DIR="/Data/to/path"

mkdir -p "${OUT_DIR}"

# 1. Gene → KO mapping (whitespace-safe)
awk '$0!~/^#/ {print $1 "\t" $3}' "${GENE_HITS}" | sort -u > "${OUT_DIR}/gene_to_KO.tsv"

# 2. Build header
HEADER=$(head -n1 "${TPM_MATRIX}" | awk '{print "KO\t" $0}')

# 3. Add KO column
awk -v OFS="\t" -v header="$HEADER" '
BEGIN{
    print header
    while((getline < "'"${OUT_DIR}/gene_to_KO.tsv"'") > 0){
        ko[$1]=$2
    }
}
NR>1{
    g=$1
    k=(g in ko)?ko[g]:"NA"
    print k, $0
}
' "${TPM_MATRIX}" > "${OUT_DIR}/gene_abundance_tpm_with_KO.tsv"

echo "[INFO] TPM matrix with KO written to: ${OUT_DIR}/gene_abundance_tpm_with_KO.tsv"



```


## 17 Plot the TPM of each gene acrross different treatment for WT and phr1phr2

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}


suppressMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(patchwork)
  library(readxl)
  library(scales)
})

# -------------------------------
# 1. Paths
# -------------------------------
abund_file <- "/Data/to/path"
meta_file  <- "/Data/to/path"
output_pdf <- "/Data/to/path"

# -------------------------------
# 2. Sample order
# -------------------------------
sample_order <- c(
  "WT_Ctl","WT_0uM","WT_10uM","WT_20uM","WT_50uM","WT_100uM","WT_200uM",
  "phr1_2_Ctl","phr1_2_0uM","phr1_2_10uM","phr1_2_20uM",
  "phr1_2_50uM","phr1_2_100uM","phr1_2_200uM"
)

WT_samples     <- grep("^WT_", sample_order, value = TRUE)
phr1_2_samples <- grep("^phr1_2_", sample_order, value = TRUE)

# -------------------------------
# 3. Metadata
# -------------------------------
ko_meta <- read_xlsx(meta_file) %>%
  distinct(KO, Gene, Nutrient)

# -------------------------------
# 4. Load abundance (NO LOG)
# -------------------------------
abund <- read.table(
  abund_file,
  header = TRUE,
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
) %>%
  filter(KO != "NA")

ko_abund <- abund %>%
  group_by(KO) %>%
  summarise(across(where(is.numeric), sum, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

colnames(ko_abund) <- gsub("_nohost$", "", colnames(ko_abund))
all_samples <- setdiff(colnames(ko_abund), "KO")

# -------------------------------
# 5. Detect replicates
# -------------------------------
get_reps <- function(samples) {
  reps <- lapply(samples, function(s) {
    cols <- grep(paste0("^", s, ".*\\d+$"), all_samples, value = TRUE)
    if (s == "WT_10uM") cols <- cols[cols != "WT_10uM_3"]
    cols
  })
  names(reps) <- samples
  reps
}

WT_reps     <- get_reps(WT_samples)
phr1_2_reps <- get_reps(phr1_2_samples)

# -------------------------------
# 6. Mean per treatment (linear)
# -------------------------------
mean_by_group <- function(rep_list) {
  out <- ko_abund %>% select(KO)
  for (s in names(rep_list)) {
    out[[s]] <- if (length(rep_list[[s]]) > 0)
      rowMeans(select(ko_abund, all_of(rep_list[[s]])), na.rm = TRUE)
    else NA_real_
  }
  out
}

WT_mean     <- mean_by_group(WT_reps)
phr1_2_mean <- mean_by_group(phr1_2_reps)

# -------------------------------
# 7. Normalize to WT_0uM (TRUE FC)
# -------------------------------
WT_0uM_ref <- WT_mean$WT_0uM
names(WT_0uM_ref) <- WT_mean$KO

normalize_fc <- function(df, ref) {
  df %>%
    mutate(across(-KO, ~ ifelse(ref > 0, . / ref, NA_real_)))
}

WT_norm     <- normalize_fc(WT_mean, WT_0uM_ref)
phr1_2_norm <- normalize_fc(phr1_2_mean, WT_0uM_ref)

# -------------------------------
# 8. Join metadata + filter genes
# -------------------------------
remove_genes <- c(
  "glpT","PiT","phoP","creC","icd","pyk","eno","gpmA","fdsB","zwf",
  "pflD","pqqA","ldh","nifQ","sdhA","acs","ackA","maeB","glcB",
  "sucA","sucB","fixJ","fixL","nifU","gltA","uhpT",
  "ugpA","ugpB","ugpC","ugpE"
)

prep_df <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    left_join(ko_meta, by = "KO") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Gene), !Gene %in% remove_genes)
}

WT_norm     <- prep_df(WT_norm)
phr1_2_norm <- prep_df(phr1_2_norm)

# -------------------------------
# 9. Custom gene order
# -------------------------------
custom_gene_order <- c(
  "pstB","pstA","pstC","pstS","phoU",
  "ugpB","ugpA","ugpE","ugpC","uhpC",
  "phoB","phoR","senX3","regX3","creB",
  "narX","narL","narQ","narP",
  "ntrB","ntrC","ntrY","ntrX",
  "narB","nasA","nasB","nirA","NIT6","nasD","nasE",
  "nifH","nifD","nifK","nifB","nifT","nifX","nifZ",
  "narG","narH","narI","napA","napB",
  "nirB","nirD","nrfA","nrfH",
  "amoA","pmoB_amoB","pmoC_amoC","hao",
  "gcd","pqqB","pqqC","pqqD","pqqE",
  "acnA/B","mdh","fumA/B","gapA","pgk",
  "sucD","frdA","ACO",
  "gdhA","ppc","PCK","pta","fdhA"
)

WT_norm$Gene     <- factor(WT_norm$Gene, levels = custom_gene_order)
phr1_2_norm$Gene <- factor(phr1_2_norm$Gene, levels = custom_gene_order)

# -------------------------------
# 10. Long format
# -------------------------------
to_long <- function(df, samples) {
  df %>%
    pivot_longer(all_of(samples), names_to = "Sample", values_to = "value") %>%
    mutate(
      Sample = factor(Sample, levels = sample_order),
      Nutrient = factor(Nutrient)
    )
}

WT_long     <- to_long(WT_norm, WT_samples)
phr1_2_long <- to_long(phr1_2_norm, phr1_2_samples)

# -------------------------------
# 11. Plot per Nutrient
# -------------------------------
plot_per_nutrient <- function(nutrient) {

  df_WT  <- WT_long     %>% filter(Nutrient == nutrient)
  df_phr <- phr1_2_long %>% filter(Nutrient == nutrient)

  lims <- range(c(df_WT$value, df_phr$value), na.rm = TRUE)
  gradient_vals <- c(lims[1], 1, lims[2])

  cols <- c("#00BFFF", "white", "#BF3EFF")

  p1 <- ggplot(df_WT, aes(Sample, Gene, fill = value)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = cols,
      values = scales::rescale(gradient_vals),
      limits = lims,
      name = paste0("Fold change\n[", nutrient, "]")
    ) +
    facet_grid(Nutrient ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA)
    ) +
    labs(title = paste("WT -", nutrient))

  p2 <- ggplot(df_phr, aes(Sample, Gene, fill = value)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(
      colors = cols,
      values = scales::rescale(gradient_vals),
      limits = lims,
      name = paste0("Fold change\n[", nutrient, "]")
    ) +
    facet_grid(Nutrient ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA)
    ) +
    labs(title = paste("phr1_2 -", nutrient))

  list(p1, p2)
}

plots <- do.call(c, lapply(unique(WT_long$Nutrient), plot_per_nutrient))

# -------------------------------
# 12. Save
# -------------------------------
ggsave(
  output_pdf,
  wrap_plots(plots, ncol = 2),
  width = 7,
  height = length(plots) * 2,
  limitsize = FALSE
)

cat(
  "[SUCCESS] Heatmaps with TRUE fold change saved:\n",
  output_pdf, "\n"
)






```







End of metagenome analysis.
